danger_signals.visual_form: Small robots with white blockheads, red bodies, and yellow appendages
danger_signals.meaning: Minions of Pinky and the Brain; direct threats to player progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct character model (appears in test levels)
danger_signals.meaning: Capture threat; defeated characters must be rescued

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards requiring specific interaction to defeat
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-specific dangers that require character-specific abilities or timing

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger enemy forms at end of stages
danger_signals.meaning: Stage completion gates; must be defeated to advance

safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration (Genesis/Game Boy versions); indicates safe pickup zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Silver and golden coins (noted in test levels)
safety_signals.meaning: Slot machine activation; grants power-ups including invincibility and character revival
Primary objective markers; hidden in safe or hard-to-reach areas
Safe navigation zone for stage selection

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Warner Bros. studio lot; various film set parodies

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Small robot minions with white/red/yellow color scheme
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot design consistency marks their organization; boss forms at stage ends
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: SNES: stealing script pages from development; Genesis: attempting memorabilia theft

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Studio infrastructure, film sets, CEO authority
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiple film genre parody stages (Western, Science Fiction, Action Movie, etc.)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Chase mechanic variant in test level 2
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Studio enforcement; appears in SNES version as capture threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Studio lot with accessible main stages; script pages scattered visibly
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pinky and the Brain remain in background antagonist role; minions populate stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple stages unlock through completion; boss designs reflect film genre parodies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters can be swapped freely; rescue mechanics activate when characters captured
Boss rush gauntlet triggers; all previous bosses reappear; final Pinky/Brain confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defeated character removed from active party; rescue stage appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining characters must complete rescue objective to restore party; lives system replaced by capture mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slot machine activates at bottom of screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Power-up availability (invincibility, character revival, coin gain/loss)

environmental_storytelling.example: Different movie sets represent thematic dangers: Western, Science Fiction, Action Movie, etc. Visual design communicates stage identity and anticipated hazard types
Pages positioned in hard-to-reach areas and revealed through object interaction; rewards exploration and character ability switching
Yakko's paddleball stuns, Wakko's mallet breaks objects/lights fuses, Dot's kiss triggers character actions; environment requires specific character to progress, communicating puzzle design visually

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Central map with stage nodes; visual layout communicates progression structure and stage availability

environmental_storytelling.example: Character faces on HUD change from smiling (healthy) to tired/unhappy/weak (injured); food pickups immediately restore visual state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Freely navigable with character-specific mechanics; can stack to reach higher platforms
Removed from party; rescue stage initiated; remaining characters must complete rescue objective

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Pinky and the Brain minion robots
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Present throughout stages; collision causes character defeat; defeated by environmental object interaction
Chases characters; capture results in character removal; cannot be defeated (TEST 2/3 behavior)
Appear at stage ends; require stage-specific object/ability interaction to defeat; reappear in Editing Room rush
Dispenses randomized power-ups; invincibility mode, character revival, or coin modification

notes: Animaniacs SNES focuses on script collection over pure platforming (distinct from Genesis version). Visual communication relies heavily on parody film aesthetics for thematic stage identity, character-ability-locked progression, and state-change feedback (character defeat = rescue mission trigger). The small robot minion design with white/red/yellow coloring serves as consistent visual faction marker. No traditional health bars in SNES version; state communicated through character capture mechanics and slot machine activation thresholds.
